project.name: COPREM OS
project.description: A fully automated personal AI operating system built by เปรม — orchestrating n8n workflows, LiteLLM routing, Postgres/pgvector, Redis, Telegram bot, and Next.js dashboard.

nodes.name: backup.yml
nodes.summary: Weekly scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (Sundays 03:00 UTC) that dumps the Postgres database, exports n8n workflows via REST API, commits the backup artifacts to the repo, and notifies เปรม via Telegram.

nodes.name: deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions deployment pipeline triggered on pushes to main affecting 03-system; runs a smoke-test job first (validates docker-compose syntax and .env keys), then deploys the Docker stack with automatic rollback and Telegram notifications on success or failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-job dependency chain (smoke-test -> deploy) with conditional rollback on failure.

nodes.name: health-check.yml
nodes.summary: Scheduled GitHub Actions workflow running every 15 minutes to verify n8n and the Cloudflare tunnel are reachable, sending Telegram alerts to เปรม when either check fails.

nodes.name: pr-check.yml
nodes.summary: Pull request validation workflow that checks docker-compose syntax, required .env keys, workflow JSON export validity, Postgres reachability, secrets scanning in the diff, and .env.example completeness before allowing a merge to main.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes a shift-left secrets scan using grep regex patterns against the PR diff.

nodes.name: 001_init_core_tables.sql
nodes.summary: Foundation migration that creates all core COPREM OS tables: users, session_store, inbox_log, task_board, okr_scoreboard, audit_log, prompt_library, and supporting tables for rate limiting, market signals, decision memory, dedup caching, security (blocked_ips), and KB sync tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Creates 15 tables and 2 indexes in a single migration; the dedup_cache table later gets superseded by migration 008.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Stores Telegram user records with chat_id, name, username, approval status, and timestamps for access control.

nodes.name: session_store
nodes.summary: Persists conversation session state including turn history, summaries, and expiry for the Telegram bot's memory across interactions.

nodes.name: inbox_log
nodes.summary: Central intake log for all incoming messages, tracking source, user, content, language detection, routing decisions (pillar/domain/agent/model), and cost metrics per request.

nodes.name: task_board
nodes.summary: Kanban-style task tracker for AI agent work items, with pillar classification, agent assignment, priority, due dates, and feedback scoring.

nodes.name: okr_scoreboard
nodes.summary: Tracks OKR progress by period and pillar, storing objective text, numeric target vs. current values, and completion percentage.

nodes.name: audit_log
nodes.summary: Append-only event sourcing log recording system events with typed event_type, source, and JSONB payload for full auditability.

nodes.name: prompt_library
nodes.summary: Version-controlled prompt store per agent, tracking active flag, average feedback score, and shadow-test scores for prompt promotion workflows.

nodes.name: decision_memory_log
nodes.summary: Records AI decisions with context, outcomes, and expiry for long-term memory and retrospective analysis by pillar.

nodes.name: 002_add_event_type_check.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent migration that adds a CHECK constraint to audit_log.event_type, enforcing the 13-value event taxonomy defined in the COPREM OS 24 Frameworks specification.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses DO $$ BEGIN ... END $$ idiom for idempotent constraint addition; references pg_constraint to guard against duplicate application.

nodes.name: 003_add_query_log.sql
nodes.summary: Adds the query_log table for recording LLM and database query history per agent, with an index on agent for fast per-agent query retrieval.

nodes.name: query_log
nodes.summary: Tracks individual query executions including agent, model, tokens, latency, cost, and the raw query text for cost and performance monitoring.

nodes.name: 004_add_system_log_json_sink.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the system_log table as a structured JSON sink for n8n workflow logs, with indexes on log level and workflow name for efficient filtering.

nodes.name: system_log
nodes.summary: Stores structured n8n workflow log entries with severity level, source workflow/node context, and arbitrary JSONB payload for flexible log aggregation.

nodes.name: 005_l4_content_library.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the L4 creative content library schema with novels, chapters (including lore tags and word count), and character_tracker tables supporting the creative writing pillar of COPREM OS.

nodes.name: novels
nodes.summary: Top-level creative project records with title, genre, status lifecycle, and timestamps.

nodes.name: chapters
nodes.summary: Chapter content store linked to a novel, carrying full text, lore tags array, and word count for creative project management.

nodes.name: character_tracker
nodes.summary: Tracks fictional characters within a novel with name, description, traits, last-seen chapter, and location for continuity management.

nodes.name: 006_enable_pgvector.sql
nodes.summary: Enables the pgvector extension and creates the memory_embeddings table with a 1536-dimension vector column and HNSW index for semantic similarity search across COPREM OS memory types.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) index via pgvector for approximate nearest-neighbor search; 1536 dimensions matches OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 output size.

nodes.name: memory_embeddings
nodes.summary: Stores vector embeddings for semantic memory retrieval, classifying memories by type (DECISION, CREATIVE, etc.), pillar, and KB source with expiry and archival support.

nodes.name: 007_slo_log.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the slo_log table for recording periodic SLO snapshots including delivery rate, average latency, HITL rate, failed tasks, and KB relevance scores.

nodes.name: slo_log
nodes.summary: Periodic SLO health snapshots storing delivery rate, latency, HITL escalation rate, failed task count, KB relevance score, and a boolean SLO pass/fail flag.

nodes.name: 008_audit_log_hash_chain.sql
nodes.summary: Adds tamper-evident hash chain columns (prev_hash, row_hash) to audit_log and installs a BEFORE INSERT trigger that computes SHA-256 chained hashes for log integrity verification.
nodes.languageNotes: Hash chain pattern: each row stores SHA256(prev_hash || id || event_type || source || timestamp), making any row modification detectable by chain verification.

nodes.name: task_queue
nodes.summary: Core task queue table for the COPREM OS job scheduler, storing task type, status, priority, payload, retry count, and scheduling timestamps to support distributed async agent execution.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion file that filters files from being sent to the Docker daemon during image builds.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template covering all required secrets and configuration keys for COPREM OS services including Postgres, n8n, Telegram, Cloudflare, Dify, Supabase, AI API keys, Redis, and Discord webhooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Groups variables by service category (DB, n8n, Telegram, Cloudflare, Dify, Supabase, AI, Redis, Discord) with 17 distinct environment variables.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration for the COPREM OS microservices stack, defining five services: n8n (workflow orchestration), PostgreSQL with pgvector (primary database), Redis (session context cache), LiteLLM (multi-key Gemini proxy), and Cloudflared (Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image to enable vector similarity search directly in Postgres; LiteLLM proxies multiple Google API keys for Gemini quota distribution.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Stores authorized Telegram users with their chat_id, display name, approval status, and timestamps for access control.

nodes.name: session_store
nodes.summary: Persistent session context store for the L1.5 Session Context Manager, holding conversation turns, session summaries, and expiry metadata.

nodes.name: inbox_log
nodes.summary: Comprehensive message audit log recording every incoming request with source, detected language, routing decisions (pillar/domain/agent/model), processing status, latency, and cost for observability.

nodes.name: task_board
nodes.summary: Task management table tracking agent-assigned tasks with priority, status lifecycle, due dates, completion timestamps, and feedback scores.

nodes.name: okr_scoreboard
nodes.summary: OKR tracking table storing objectives and key results per pillar with target, current value, and percentage completion for the cybernetic feedback loop.

nodes.name: audit_log
nodes.summary: Security audit log capturing all events with event type, source IP, payload, and timestamp for the L7 security and compliance layer.

nodes.name: prompt_library
nodes.summary: Versioned prompt storage for all agents with active flag, average feedback score, and shadow score to support the L5 cybernetic prompt optimization loop.

nodes.name: novels
nodes.summary: Novel registry with genre, status, and summary for the creative writing pillar.

nodes.name: chapters
nodes.summary: Chapter storage linked to novels with content, lore tags, word count, and status for structured story management.

nodes.name: dedup_cache
nodes.summary: Message deduplication cache storing hashes of processed messages to prevent duplicate processing in the routing layer.

nodes.name: rate_limit_registry
nodes.summary: Per-provider API rate limit tracker recording remaining requests, reset time, and throttle state for the LiteLLM multi-key Gemini proxy.

nodes.name: config.yaml
nodes.summary: LiteLLM proxy configuration defining a model list with multiple Gemini API key slots (eilinaire, jooshdathumlong, mahittisak, sunsetsol, rlie, peabantid) plus Groq fallback, router settings for load balancing, and general proxy settings including rate limiting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multiple Google API keys mapped to the same model name to enable LiteLLM's round-robin routing, effectively multiplying Gemini free-tier quota across API keys.

nodes.name: exports
nodes.summary: Directory placeholder for exported n8n workflow JSON files, mounted read-only into the n8n container at /home/node/exports.

nodes.name: decision_memory_log
nodes.summary: Persistent decision memory store recording agent decisions with context, outcome, pillar classification, expiry, and archive status for the L3 hybrid memory layer.

nodes.name: failed_tasks_db
nodes.summary: Failed task retry queue tracking error messages, retry counts, and last retry timestamps for the L5 cybernetic feedback recovery loop.

nodes.name: quarantine_db
nodes.summary: Quarantine store holding agent outputs that failed validation, with raw output, failure reason, and review/resolve flags for human oversight.

nodes.name: autonomous_loop.py
nodes.summary: Core autonomous task execution engine that polls Postgres for pending tasks, dispatches them to typed handlers (chat, analysis, agent-handoff, report, kb-embed), manages retry logic, and sends Telegram notifications on failures.

nodes.name: fetch_pending_tasks
nodes.summary: Queries Postgres via docker exec for pending/scheduled tasks and parses raw psql output into structured task dicts.

nodes.name: call_litellm
nodes.summary: Sends chat completion requests to LiteLLM with retry on rate-limit headers and exponential back-off, returning the response content string.

nodes.name: handle_analysis
nodes.summary: Handles 'analysis' task type by calling LiteLLM with a prompt, optionally notifying via Telegram, and spawning a follow-up task when chaining is configured.

nodes.name: handle_agent_handoff
nodes.summary: Handles agent-handoff tasks by creating a new child task assigned to the target agent with priority and delay parameters.

nodes.name: process_task
nodes.summary: Dispatches a task to the appropriate handler, marks it done or retries on transient errors, and marks it permanently failed after max retries.

nodes.name: run_loop
nodes.summary: Main daemon loop: writes a PID file, installs signal handlers, and polls for pending tasks in a continuous cycle with configurable sleep intervals.

nodes.name: gemini_router.py
nodes.summary: Rotates across multiple Gemini API keys with per-key throttle tracking (both rate-limit and daily-quota), calling the first available key and falling back gracefully.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main routing function that iterates available non-throttled Gemini keys, attempts a generation call, and handles quota exhaustion by marking the key throttled.

nodes.name: agent_eval.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic CLI that queries Postgres task and agent tables to report task throughput, latency percentiles, error rates, and per-agent performance over a configurable time window.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Executes all evaluation sections: task summary, latency stats, error breakdown, per-agent metrics, and recommendation output, printing a structured report to stdout.

nodes.name: build_master_context.py
nodes.summary: Generates a consolidated master_context.md file by walking the project directory tree and concatenating key configuration and documentation files.

nodes.name: embed_kb.py
nodes.summary: Pulls knowledge-base document segments from Dify, generates embeddings via LiteLLM, and upserts them into a Postgres pgvector table for semantic search.

nodes.name: pg_upsert
nodes.summary: Upserts a knowledge-base segment with its vector embedding into Postgres via docker exec psql, handling ON CONFLICT updates.

nodes.name: fix_credentials.py
nodes.summary: Re-encrypts n8n database credentials using AES-CBC with OpenSSL EVP key derivation and updates the encrypted values directly in Postgres after restarts.

nodes.name: encrypt
nodes.summary: Encrypts a credential dict using AES-256-CBC with OpenSSL-compatible salted key derivation, returning a base64-encoded ciphertext string.

nodes.name: health_check.sh
nodes.summary: Comprehensive system health checker that verifies all COPREM services (n8n, Postgres, Redis, LiteLLM, Telegram webhook, dashboard, autonomous loop) and auto-fixes credential drift, zombie containers, and misconfigured webhooks, writing results to SYSTEM_STATE.md.

nodes.name: latency_report.sh
nodes.summary: Queries the v_latency_by_layer Postgres view to report the top 5 slowest request layers across the COPREM stack.

nodes.name: post_restart.sh
nodes.summary: Post-restart recovery script that waits for n8n, syncs Postgres credentials, stops zombie containers, activates the Telegram webhook workflow, ensures the user record exists, starts Ollama, and launches the autonomous loop and Next.js dashboard.

nodes.name: run_autonomous_loop.sh
nodes.summary: Thin launchd wrapper that bypasses macOS sandbox restrictions by exec-ing the autonomous_loop.py directly via an absolute Python path.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses exec to replace the shell process, required for macOS launchd compatibility.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: One-time local setup script that installs Git hooks, creates .env from the example template, and verifies Docker availability before the first stack launch.

nodes.name: sqlite_io.py
nodes.summary: SQLite I/O library for the autonomous loop, providing schema initialization, row insertion, table reads, and arbitrary query execution against a vault-specific database file.

nodes.name: init_schema
nodes.summary: Creates all SQLite tables (tasks, agents, knowledge, sessions, logs) via an executescript, initializing the full autonomous loop schema.

nodes.name: start_coprem.sh
nodes.summary: Top-level COPREM launcher that starts Docker Compose services, the Next.js dashboard, the autonomous loop daemon, and opens the dashboard in Chrome app mode.

nodes.name: stop_coprem.sh
nodes.summary: Gracefully stops the COPREM OS stack by terminating the autonomous loop daemon and bringing down Docker Compose services.

nodes.name: sync_daemon.py
nodes.summary: Bi-directional Obsidian vault sync daemon that mirrors English markdown files to a Thai vault with localized labels and headers, supporting check, sync, clean, and watch modes.

nodes.name: mode_sync
nodes.summary: Syncs missing and stale English markdown files to the Thai vault mirror, creating or updating Thai mirror files as needed.

nodes.name: mode_watch
nodes.summary: Continuous watch loop that periodically runs mode_check and triggers mode_sync when stale or missing files are detected.

nodes.name: sync_docs.sh
nodes.summary: Shell wrapper that invokes sync_daemon.py in sync mode and optionally cleans orphaned Thai vault entries.

nodes.name: sync_validator.py
nodes.summary: Validates vault sync integrity by computing MD5 checksums of markdown files and comparing them against SQLite audit records, reporting discrepancies and orphans.

nodes.name: SyncValidator
nodes.summary: Encapsulates vault sync audit logic: computes file checksums, queries SQLite for tracked state, and emits a discrepancy report.

nodes.name: verify_log_integrity.py
nodes.summary: Queries Postgres agent_logs and verifies chained SHA-256 hash integrity of log entries, reporting any breaks in the tamper-evident chain.

nodes.name: apply_migrations.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent migration runner that applies SQL migrations 002-004 to the COPREM Postgres database via docker exec, then verifies constraints and tables exist.

nodes.name: temp_embed_rs_lifestyle.py
nodes.summary: One-off script that extracts Royal Shammi lifestyle content segments from Postgres, generates embeddings via LiteLLM, and upserts them into the pgvector knowledge base.

nodes.name: get_summaries
nodes.summary: Queries multiple Postgres tables (products, benefits, usage, reviews) and assembles text segments for embedding into the knowledge base.

nodes.name: coprem
nodes.summary: Main CLI entry point for COPREM OS, providing commands to run tasks via n8n webhooks, query Postgres for status/cost/memory, manage agent routing, trigger workflows, and execute an emergency killswitch.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash script using a case/esac dispatch table; directly wraps both docker-compose postgres and curl webhook calls, making it the single operator interface to the entire COPREM OS stack.

layers.name: Knowledge Base
layers.description: Structured personal and brand knowledge files in 02-knowledge/ (brand, categories, personal profiles, work references) that feed the AI embedding and retrieval pipeline.

layers.name: System Documentation
layers.description: COPREM OS architecture blueprints, decision logs, skills playbooks, root-level agent instructions (CLAUDE.md, INDEX.md, STATUS.md), and monitoring/security specs.

layers.name: Project Documents
layers.description: Personal creative project documentation under 01-projects/ covering the eilinaire brand, ego-era fiction, music, peabuntid, and trading workstreams.

layers.name: Archive
layers.description: Historical versions of workflows, configs, and Thai-mirror documents from previous COPREM OS iterations (v7.2, v8.2) preserved for reference in _archive/.

layers.name: Outputs & Runtime
layers.description: Generated HTML command-center artifacts, Ollama model manifests, runtime log files, and AI memory markdown files produced during COPREM OS operation.

tour.title: System Overview
tour.description: COPREM Master Context is the single-page snapshot of the entire operating system — it maps every active layer, workflow status, and the core message loop (Telegram → n8n → LiteLLM → Telegram). Reading this first gives you the mental model you need to make sense of every file that follows. Think of it as the cockpit overview before you inspect individual instruments.

tour.title: 10-Layer Architecture Blueprint
tour.description: Blueprint v8.3 is the canonical design document that defines all 10 architectural layers, from L1 message ingestion through L7 security and L10 self-optimization. It documents decisions about service boundaries, failure modes, and upgrade paths. After the context overview in Step 1, this blueprint reveals the deliberate reasoning behind why the system is split the way it is, and what each numbered layer is responsible for.

tour.title: Architectural Decisions
tour.description: decisions.md records the key architectural choices made during COPREM OS's evolution — such as why LiteLLM was chosen over direct provider SDKs, why n8n handles orchestration instead of custom code, and how Redis and Postgres complement each other. Understanding these decisions before reading the infrastructure files will prevent the "why not just use X?" confusion that often slows down codebase exploration.

tour.title: Stack Entry Point
tour.description: start_coprem.sh is the single command that brings the entire COPREM OS to life. It orchestrates startup sequencing — ensuring Postgres and Redis are healthy before n8n initializes, and LiteLLM is ready before workflows begin accepting messages. Tracing what this script calls reveals the dependency order of the entire infrastructure layer and is the best map of the system's boot-time contract.
tour.languageLesson: Shell startup scripts often encode implicit dependency graphs through their sequencing logic — the order of service health checks and wait loops is architecture documentation in executable form.

tour.title: Infrastructure Orchestration
tour.description: The Docker Compose file defines every service that COPREM OS runs: n8n for workflow automation, Postgres with pgvector for semantic memory, Redis for session caching, LiteLLM as the LLM gateway, and Cloudflared for secure external access. This is the highest fan-in file in the codebase — nearly every other component depends on the services declared here. The network topology, volume mounts, and port mappings here directly shape how every other layer communicates.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose service dependencies defined with `depends_on` and health checks enforce startup ordering. Named volumes persist data across container restarts, separating stateful data (Postgres, Redis) from stateless application containers.

tour.title: LLM Routing Layer
tour.description: litellm/config.yaml is the most-depended-upon configuration file in the project (fan-in of 6). It defines the routing rules that send different types of requests to different LLM backends — Groq for fast inference, six Gemini variants for different capability tiers, and local Ollama for offline or cost-sensitive tasks. Every workflow and the Jeff agent persona from Step 7 ultimately resolve their AI calls through this single routing table.
tour.languageLesson: LiteLLM acts as an OpenAI-compatible proxy, so all upstream callers use a unified API regardless of which provider is selected. This allows model swaps without changing any workflow or agent code — only this config file needs updating.

tour.title: Jeff Agent Persona
tour.description: jeff-agent.yml defines the main AI persona that end users interact with through Telegram. It sets the system prompt, tone, tool access, and model preference for Jeff — the personal assistant identity that COPREM OS presents. This config is invoked by WF01 (the inbox receiver) and routes its LLM calls through the LiteLLM layer defined in Step 6, making it the bridge between human-facing behavior and infrastructure-level routing.

tour.title: Core Workflow — WF01 Inbox Receiver
tour.description: WF01 is the heartbeat of COPREM OS. Every inbound Telegram message flows through this n8n workflow: it applies L7 security checks, performs L3 semantic search against Postgres/pgvector to retrieve relevant memory, calls the Jeff agent (which routes through LiteLLM), and sends the reply back to Telegram. Reading the workflow JSON alongside its specification doc reveals the exact node graph — webhook trigger, security filter, memory lookup, AI call, response node.
tour.languageLesson: n8n workflows are stored as JSON graphs where each node has a type, parameters, and connection list. The JSON export is the source of truth for workflow logic — it can be imported directly into any n8n instance, making the workflow version-controllable alongside the rest of the codebase.

tour.title: Database Schema & Memory Layer
tour.description: schemas.sql defines all 15+ tables in the coprem_os schema, including the users table (with its approval flow), conversation history, semantic memory chunks, OKR tracking, and audit logs. The pgvector extension on the memory_chunks table is what enables the semantic search step inside WF01 — embeddings stored here are queried by cosine similarity to retrieve contextually relevant memories for each incoming message.
tour.languageLesson: pgvector adds a `vector` column type to Postgres and exposes operators like `<=>` (cosine distance) and `<->` (L2 distance). This lets semantic similarity search run inside the same transactional database as the rest of the application data, avoiding a separate vector store service.

tour.title: KB Embedding Pipeline
tour.description: embed_kb.py is the script that populates the vector memory layer. It reads documents from the knowledge base, generates embeddings via LiteLLM, and upserts them into the pgvector table defined in Step 9. Running this script is what gives Jeff the ability to answer questions about past conversations, personal notes, and work documents — the semantic search in WF01 is only as good as what embed_kb.py has indexed.

tour.title: Autonomous Task Daemon
tour.description: autonomous_loop.py is the background process that handles everything COPREM OS does without a human prompt — morning briefs, OKR reviews, KB sync cycles, health pings, and self-optimization runs. It orchestrates calls into n8n workflows and into embed_kb.py on a schedule, making COPREM OS proactive rather than purely reactive. This daemon is what distinguishes a personal AI operating system from a simple chatbot.

tour.title: Credential Management
tour.description: fix_credentials.py solves a concrete operational constraint: n8n encrypts its stored credentials using an encryption key, and after a container restart those encrypted blobs can become invalid if the key rotates. This script re-encrypts both primary credentials against the current key so workflows can reconnect to Postgres, Telegram, and other services without manual UI intervention. It is a critical operational tool that you need to run after every stack restart.

tour.title: Monitoring Dashboard
tour.description: CopremDashboard.tsx is the Next.js React component that surfaces the system's live state — active workflows, recent messages, memory stats, LLM usage metrics, and OKR progress. It queries the Postgres schema from Step 9 directly, providing a human-readable view of everything the autonomous loop and WF01 are doing. This is the observation layer that closes the feedback loop, letting เปรม see COPREM OS's activity at a glance.
tour.languageLesson: React components that serve as dashboard shells typically use polling (setInterval + fetch) or WebSocket subscriptions to keep live metrics updated. Separating data-fetching hooks from presentational components makes it easier to swap the data source without rewriting the UI.
